title: 'Women will respond': Congress slams govt over 'delay' in 33% quota; says implement it at once
content: Congress Gurugram District President Vardhan Yadav said the issue of women's reservation is not merely political, but rather related to social justice and equality

title: T.N. Assembly elections: Over 50 lakh more voters exercise their franchise this time compared to 2024 Lok Sabha polls
content: In the State, people’s interest in polling process has always been higher for Assembly elections when compared to Lok Sabha polls, show data from elections held over the past 20 years; parties carry out a more intensive mobilisation for State polls

title: SFI seeks action against ‘harassment’ of medical PG students
content: All 12 PG doctors in the department have lodged a complaint with the Principal, alleging humiliation by two faculty members

title: Officials draw flak over alleged administrative lapses in Kozhikode Corporation
content: Councillors across ruling and Opposition benches demand action against officials over recurring lapses. Mayor says the Corporation will not shield those found guilty and promises strict action.

title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah pitches Kashmir as ideal shooting destination to South Indian filmmakers
content: The Chief Minister highlighted J&K’s unparalleled natural beauty, diverse landscapes, and rich cultural heritage, ranging from serene valleys and snow-clad mountains to vibrant traditions and local craftsmanship

title: Vehicles stranded after avalanche hits Srinagar-Kargil highway
content: Snow sheaths started sliding into a major avalanche in the afternoon at Shaitan Nallah, which connects Sonamarg in the Kashmir Valley with Drass in Ladakh, officials say

title: Artists get creative at ‘Articulate Gunturian’
content: Their artworks showcase varied themes such as cultural heritage, women’s safety, festivals, social stigma, nature and rural life

title: Residents stage protest, seek house sites in Mangalagiri
content: Protesters demand distribution of house sites, construction support, and issuance of pattas to families living on assigned land

title: Tagged Olive Ridley turtles from T.N. reach Sri Lanka Dome, reveal migration patterns
content: Considered one of the most productive regions in the Indian Ocean, the Sri Lanka Dome phenomenon typically begins in May, peaks in July, and declines by September

title: Four booked for child marriage in Kasaragod
content: The police said the case was registered after the Child Marriage Prohibition Officer received a complaint that a minor girl from Valiyaparamba had been married off

title: Calls to do away with elephants, fireworks in temple festivals get louder in Kerala
content: In the aftermath of the recent fireworks tragedy at Mundathikode in Thrissur, social media campaigns are gathering strength seeking to move away from the use of high-intensity gunpowder displays as well as elephants during festivities

title: War of words erupts over girl’s death in Ghazipur, U.P.; Rahul Gandhi demands immediate justice
content: Congress General Secretary and Lok Sabha MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra alleged that atrocities against women in U.P. reached its peak under the BJP rule

title: ECI suspends police officials in Bengal; Trinamool alleges strongroom breach
content: The poll body has ordered the suspension of five police officials at Diamond Harbour over ‘serious misconduct’ and ‘failure to maintain neutrality’ during Assembly polls

title: Dinesh Trivedi | A man for all seasons
content: A politician with close ties to West Bengal, the BJP leader heads to Dhaka as India’s envoy, with a mandate to reset ties with the neighbour

title: NHAI demolishes top portion of stormwater drain to create more road space in Vellore
content: A new drain will be built corresponding to the existing road level to allow more vehicles, especially buses, to navigate easily on the carriageway

title: Seven e-bikes catch fire near Ranipet town
content: The police said that a mechanic was checking the condition of batteries at the shop when the explosion happened

title: March to Manipur CM’s house foiled; 22 injured
content: They took out the march demanding action against those behind the April 7 bomb attack that killed two children

title: Concern over air quality dip in Mysuru
content: Factors contributing to poor air quality include smoking, burning of waste materials and garbage and vehicular emissions among others

title: Restoration work at Hindustan Petroleum’s Rajasthan refinery expected to complete in 3-4 weeks
content: HPCL also informed that the trial production of liquified petroleum products, motor spirit, high-speed diesel and naphtha is expected to start within May this year

title: Census 2027: States asked to identify vulnerable areas, keep biased enumerators out
content: RG&CCI Mritunjay Kumar Narayan asks officials to ensure that vulnerable sections of people, including those affected by communal, political and social tensions are counted as any omission may lead to litigations in future

title: Congress attacks RSS, Modi over Ram Madhav’s comments on Indo-U.S. ties
content: In a social media post, Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi says Mr. Madhav has revealed the RSS’s fake nationalism and the nature of servitude to the U.S.

title: U.S. sanctions waiver on Chabahar port ends on April 26, could signal end of 23-year-old connectivity project
content: Officials say they are discussing a possible transfer of IPGL-subsidiary’s stake to local Iranian company temporarily to avoid sanctions

title: No Iran-US peace talks 2.0? How Pakistan's bid to mediate a breakthrough fell short
content: Pakistan's efforts to mediate US-Iran talks faltered as Tehran refused direct engagement, conveying demands through Islamabad. US President Trump subsequently canceled his delegation's visit, citing a lack of meaningful progress. The diplomatic deadlock persists, with Iran shifting its focus to Oman and Russia.

title: 'Too much time wasted': Trump scraps US envoys' visit to Pak, says Iran can 'call' for talks
content: US President Donald Trump canceled a planned trip by his envoys to Pakistan to meet Iranian leaders, citing wasted travel time and confusion within Iran's leadership. He asserted the US holds the upper hand and urged Iran to initiate talks. This decision has diminished Pakistan's role in facilitating US-Iran discussions.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: No ballot papers, no ink, no boxes: Inside Gaza's first election in two decades
content: Gaza witnessed a historic moment as Palestinians engaged in their first local elections in over two decades, marking a tentative step toward reconciling the fragmented political landscape between Gaza and the West Bank. Though hampered by Israeli-imposed restrictions, officials creatively utilised whatever materials they could muster to ensure the elections went ahead.

title: Head in hands: Vipraj Nigam left stunned as Karun Nair drops Iyer twice - Watch
content: PBKS captain Shreyas Iyer got two major lifelines during the chase and made Delhi pay with a 71-run knock off 36 balls.

title: 'Humiliating defeat': Iran reminds US of 46-yr-old failure - what was Op Eagle Claw?
content: Iran's judiciary chief warned the US of repeating "Isfahan and Tabas" incidents if tensions escalate in the Persian Gulf. This warning coincides with the 46th anniversary of the failed US Operation Eagle Claw in 1980, a mission to rescue American hostages in Tehran that ended in disaster and casualties.

title: KPMG to lay off 10% of US audit team after failed retirement efforts
content: KPMG is set to cut approximately 10% of its US audit partners after voluntary retirement schemes failed to yield desired results. The accounting giant, one of the Big Four, cited a need to align its partnership size with current business demands.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office day 38: Film slowly inches towards Rs 1800 cr worldwide
content: In a stunning display of cinematic prowess, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' has captivated audiences, amassing nearly Rs 1800 crore globally. Surpassing the Rs 1700 crore mark, it now stands as the third highest-grossing Indian film. By its sixth week, it continues to fend off stiff competition, raking in remarkable box office figures both at home and abroad. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Microsoft’s former CEO says “I feel silly,” shares letter to judge over fintech loss
content: Former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer is furious after losing $60 million to fintech founder Joseph Sanberg's Aspiration Partners. Sanberg defrauded investors with false environmental claims. Ballmer's reputation suffered, leading to a public campaign against him and an NBA investigation. Sanberg's plea deal and sentencing are pending.

title: 'Torn pants to Rs 100 cr house': Swati Maliwal's 'traitor' jibe at Kejriwal after AAP exit
content: Former aide to Arvind Kejriwal and now BJP leader Swati Maliwal has accused the ex-Delhi CM of hypocrisy, claiming he began his political journey in torn pants with a two-rupee pen and a dilapidated car, but after coming to power built a house worth Rs 100 crore.She also criticised the AAP's governance in Punjab. The remarks follow a significant party exodus.

title: Ronaldo’s 3 final career goals before retirement – Messi already ahead
content: Cristiano Ronaldo is entering the final phase of his career with three clear goals still driving him forward: a first World Cup title in 2026, the pursuit of 1,000 career goals, and the rare chance of playing alongside his son at Al Nassr. Even at 41, he remains central for club and country, shaping both legacy and milestones.

title: Patrick Mahomes & Brittany Mahomes hit $325m valuation amid recovery
content: Patrick Mahomes, the Kansas City Chiefs’ quarterback, is focused on his recovery from his brutal injuries as his wife, Brittany Mahomes stands by his side through all the difficult times. While both Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, have preferred to be private about the star player’s recovery, the high profile couple recently received an update worth a whopping $325 million.

title: Titans uncover affair? Mike Vrabel exit linked to Russini drama
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has taken some time off from work to be with his family as his alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini continue to get worse every day. Though the New England Patriots have not yet hinted at firing Mike Vrabel ahead of the new season, it seems his time with the Tennessee Titans is being viewed differently.

title: Sooryavanshi overtakes Kohli, KL Rahul in Orange Cap race after sensational ton
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has claimed the IPL 2026 Orange Cap, surpassing Virat Kohli with a blistering 103 off 37 balls. His explosive innings, featuring a 15-ball fifty and a 36-ball century, propelled Rajasthan Royals to a massive total. Sooryavanshi now leads the charts with 357 runs at an exceptional strike rate.

title: Tom Cruise fitness at 63: Inside his 1,200-calorie stunt diet
content: Even in his 60s, Tom Cruise exemplifies peak fitness, tailored for high-octane action roles. His regimen marries the thrill of outdoor sports like paragliding and hiking with rigorous strength training and endurance exercises.

title: Malaria free by 2030? Cases down by over 80% but elimination a big challenge
content: Monsoon brings romanticized scenes but also a public health reality of increased malaria risk, especially for vulnerable groups. Despite significant national declines, malaria persists in localized pockets due to environmental and socio-economic factors. Climate change further complicates transmission patterns, necessitating sustained surveillance and targeted interventions for elimination.

title: Watch: AAP workers write 'Gaddar' outside Rajinder Gupta's house after he joins BJP
content: Aam Aadmi Party workers protested outside Rajya Sabha MP Rajinder Gupta's Ludhiana residence, defacing walls and burning his effigy after he joined the BJP. This follows AAP MP Raghav Chadha's announcement of a split, citing the party's deviation from principles. Chadha claimed over two-thirds of AAP MPs supported the move, marking a significant setback for the party.

title: Lungi Ngidi injury: Why DC picked Vipraj over Chameera as concussion substitute
content: Ngidi suffered a head injury while trying to take a catch of Punjab Kings opener Priyansh Arya at the Arun Jaitley Stadium on Saturday and had to be taken off the field in an ambulance before being shifted to hospital.

title: Watch: Gunfire, blasts rock Mali's capital; India issues advisory
content: Fresh violence erupted across Mali early Saturday, with "unidentified armed terrorist groups" targeting multiple locations including the capital Bamako, its international airport, and the key military town of Kati. Attacks also spread to northern cities, with the Azawad Liberation Front reportedly entering Kidal. The defense minister's house in Kati was reportedly hit.

title: Watch: Epstein files video showing Trump projected on to building in Washington DC
content: A provocative protest projected an old video of Donald Trump with Jeffrey Epstein onto the Washington Hilton, the venue for the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The display highlighted Trump's past association with the disgraced financier and criticised the administration's handling of the Epstein files, with messages suggesting complicity and a cover-up.

title: AAP implosion: BJP gains RS numbers, but will it be advantage Congress in Punjab?
content: The AAP, as expected, reacted very strongly to this development and accused the BJP of “Operation Lotus”, a term used to define saffron party engineered defections of political rivals. AAP leader Sanjay Singh alleged that central agencies like the Enforcement Directorate (ED) and the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) were misused to pressure and break away AAP leaders. AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal accused the BJP of betraying Punjab, which is ruled by AAP and goes to assembly polls next year.

title: Record shattered! Punjab Kings chase 264 vs Delhi Capitals - here are IPL’s highest successful run chases
content: Punjab Kings achieved the highest successful run chase in IPL history, chasing down a colossal 264 against Delhi Capitals. Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh provided a blistering start, followed by a match-winning unbeaten 71 from captain Shreyas Iyer, securing a six-wicket victory with seven balls to spare.

title: Watch: China navy's video hints its 4th aircraft carrier will be nuclear powered
content: China's potential fourth aircraft carrier, possibly named 'He Jian' and hinted to be nuclear-powered, signals a significant advancement in its naval capabilities. This development, showcased in the film 'Into The Deep', aligns with China's transition to a blue-water navy and its expanding global maritime ambitions.

title: Court quashes FIR against Ranveer Singh, directs him to visit Chamundi temple within four weeks
content: Actor Ranveer Singh's plea to quash a criminal complaint over his 'Kantara' mimicry was accepted by the Karnataka High Court. He assured the court he would visit the Chamundi Hill Temple in Mysore as an apology for his controversial remarks, with the court to record this commitment before closing the plea.

title: 3 dead, 17 houses burnt in 24 hrs, fresh clashes: Why Manipur is on the boil again
content: Protests erupted in Manipur as thousands demanded peace, clashing with security forces near the chief minister's residence. Demonstrators, organised by COCOMI, sought justice for children killed in a recent bomb blast and support for the displaced. The state grapples with escalating ethnic violence, with recent clashes claiming lives and displacing many.

title: Drill, sanction, control: Inside the oil economics driving Trump 2.0
content: US President Trump's second term prioritizes oil, leveraging energy dominance for global influence. Campaign funding from oil interests aligns with policies boosting domestic production and easing regulations. Sanctions on rivals and geopolitical events like the Hormuz crisis engineer demand for American exports, impacting nations like India and reshaping global energy markets.

title: Scary scenes! Lungi Ngidi taken off in ambulance after suffering head injury
content: The incident happened in the third over of Punjab Kings' chase when Arya played a slash at a full ball on the stumps off Axar Patel. The ball went high towards mid-off, where Ngidi ran back to complete the catch.

title: Raghav Chadha loses over 1m followers, flooded with hate comments after joining BJP
content: Raghav Chadha's Instagram following plummeted by over 1 million after his switch from AAP to BJP. Once a youth icon, his digital persona faced backlash, with followers expressing disappointment and betrayal. Allegations of deleting critical posts against the BJP surfaced, which Chadha denied. His appeal previously rested on addressing everyday issues, a stark contrast to his current political move.

title: NFL trade rumor: Falcons eye $63M ex-patriots star despite issues
content: The Atlanta Falcons have failed to perform well in the last couple of seasons as it has missed qualifying in the playoffs for the eighth time in a row, sparking concern among fans about it. With just 8 wins and 9 losses in the last two consecutive seasons, all focus is on bouncing back in the new season.

title: 'Gunfire everywhere': Explosions heard near Mali international airport, military base
content: Mali faced coordinated attacks on Saturday. Gunfire and explosions were heard in the capital Bamako and near a military camp housing Russian mercenaries. Unidentified terrorist groups targeted several positions. Fighting continued in the capital and other regions. Security forces responded to the unfolding situation. Al-Qaeda affiliate JNIM is suspected of involvement.

title: Handwritten letters, human chain: How Mojtaba Khamenei controls Iran from the shadows
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is reportedly leading the country through a secret "human chain" of handwritten letters to evade US and Israeli detection.  This comes amid unprecedented security measures, with senior officials avoiding visits due to assassination fears following a reported US-Israel strike that gravely injured him.

title: IPL: KL Rahul scripts history, becomes first Indian to…
content: KL Rahul achieved a historic milestone with an unbeaten 152 for Delhi Capitals against Punjab Kings. This remarkable innings is the third-highest individual score in IPL history. Rahul's performance, aided by dropped catches, powered Delhi to a commanding total. He partnered with Nitish Rana for a significant second-wicket stand, setting a new franchise record.

title: IPL 2026: KL Rahul creates IPL history with unique 3-franchise century feat
content: KL Rahul unleashed a sensational unbeaten 152, a record for an Indian in the IPL, as Delhi Capitals posted a colossal 264/2 against Punjab Kings. Supported by Nitish Rana's 91, their 220-run partnership powered DC to their highest-ever total. Rahul's fiery knock featured 16 fours and nine sixes, marking his fastest century.

title: 'Left out of disgust, not fear': Raghav Chadha attacks AAP over Sheesh Mahal 2.0
content: Raghav Chadha, a prominent AAP leader, has quit the party citing disappointment and disgust, not fear, over alleged corruption and a "Sheesh Mahal" controversy. He stated that seven MPs are leaving together, believing the AAP has fallen into compromised hands. Chadha's departure follows weeks of escalating tensions and his removal as deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha.

title: What's cooking in pantry? Bite into 'adulterated' truth
content: Food adulteration in India poses a significant public health crisis, with contaminated milk, paneer, and processed foods causing severe health issues and even deaths. Recent incidents highlight widespread violations, including a deadly milk contamination in Andhra Pradesh and large seizures of adulterated paneer in Surat, underscoring the urgent need for stricter regulations and enforcement.

title: IPL: KL Rahul, Nitish Rana break all-time record vs Punjab Kings
content: KL Rahul and Nitish Rana delivered a batting spectacle, forging a record 150+ run partnership for Delhi Capitals against Punjab Kings. Rahul's explosive century, coupled with Rana's rapid half-century and a massive 28-run over, powered DC to a commanding position. This dominant display rewrote DC's record books.

title: Iran war could push over 32m people into poverty; UN warns of 'triple shock'
content: A United Nations report warns the Iran war's economic fallout could plunge over 32 million people into poverty globally. Developing nations face the brunt of this crisis, grappling with surging energy and food prices. The conflict reverses development gains, pushing vulnerable populations back into destitution. Urgent global support is needed to aid these hardest-hit countries and prevent lasting damage.

title: IPL: KL Rahul goes berserk with 47-ball ton, equals Gayle to enter elite list
content: KL Rahul smashed a brilliant IPL century, his sixth, off just 47 balls against Punjab Kings.  Capitalizing on two dropped catches, Rahul, supported by Nitish Rana's unbeaten 72, forged a record 185-run partnership. This landmark knock places Rahul among IPL's top centurions and highlights his unique achievement of scoring centuries for three different franchises.

title: All Not Well In Uddhav Sena? MPs' 'Courtesy' Meetings Spark Exodus Buzz
content: Sources familiar with recent meetings among the MPs in Delhi point to a deeper issue -- an alleged communication gap between the party's leadership and its parliamentary wing.

title: Gunmen Attack Several Locations Across Mali's Capital
content: Mali's army said in a statement that "unidentified armed terrorist groups targeted certain locations and barracks" in Bamako and that soldiers were "engaged in eliminating the attackers."

title: China Hints Its 4th Aircraft Carrier Will Be Nuclear-Powered
content: The video film 'Into The Deep' was released on Wednesday to mark the 77th anniversary of the founding of the People's Liberation Army Navy showcased Chinese navy's transition from maritime ambitions and coastal defences.

title: Will It, Won't It? Trump's Big Move Casts Shadow Over US-Iran Peace Talks
content: According to Fox News, Trump said that he told the envoys not to get on an "18-hour flight" while they were getting ready to leave. He said that he does not want his officials to "sit around talking about nothing".

title: Survival, Legal Fight, Punjab Battle: What's Next for AAP After MP Exodus
content: The AAP has said it will seek the disqualification of the seven MPs and take the legal route if there is a delay.

title: "Blindness": Court Asks Tamil Nadu To Roll Out Transgender Rehabilitation Plan
content: The court reiterated that transgender persons are protected under Articles 14, 15, 16 and 21, which guarantee equality and the right to live with dignity.

title: 4 Killed In Israeli Airstrikes On Lebanon Amid Fragile Ceasefire
content: Israel and Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah have continued to clash in southern Lebanon despite reduced hostilities due to the ceasefire.

title: Delhi Records Hottest Day Of Year At 42.8 Degrees, Yellow Alert Issued
content: Across Delhi, the impact is already being felt through blistering afternoons and unusually warm nights, with temperatures well above normal.

title: 'Iran Can Call Us Anytime': Trump After Cancelling Top Officials' Pak Trip
content: Trump says he has cancelled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's trip to Pak for Iran talks: News agency AFP citing Fox News

title: 15-Year-Old Boy Killed After Speeding Tempo Hits Bike In Delhi: Cops
content: A 15-year-old boy was killed on Saturday evening after an overspeeding tempo hit his motorcycle from behind in Shalimar Bagh here, police said.

title: Raghav Chadha Loses 1.1 Million Instagram Followers After AAP Rebellion
content: As of now, his follower count has declined to 13.1 million, indicating that nearly 1.1 million users have unfollowed him within just 24 hours.

title: Fabulous Lives Of Cartel Wives: Women Masterminds Of Latin Drug Businesses
content: The recent killing of the world's most-wanted traffickers, Nemesio "El Mencho" Oseguera Cervantes, in a daring operation by the Mexican military has revealed that sometimes it is the cartel women that you need to watch out for.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iran FM Says 'Yet To See If The US Is Truly Serious About Diplomacy'
content: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: The standoff between the US and Iran has effectively choked off nearly all exports through the strait -- where 20 per cent of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime -- with no end in sight.

title: Russia Targets Ukraine In Overnight Drone Attack, Parts Found In Romania
content: While drone fragments have routinely fallen on Romania, Saturday marked the first time property had been damaged.

title: Woman, 30, Dies By Suicide After Argument With Husband In Gurugram: Cops
content: A woman allegedly died by suicide after hanging herself with a scarf on Saturday following an argument with her husband in Palda village, police said.

title: AAP Rebel Met Arvind Kejriwal Before Raghav Chadha Shocker. What He Told Him
content: Vikramjit Singh Sahney revealed that he met with AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal on Wednesday and informed him about his plan to join the BJP.

title: India's First Private Orbital Rocket Vikram-1 Inches Closer To Launch
content: If Vikram-1 succeeds, it could create history as India's first privately developed rocket to place satellites into orbit.

title: 2 Missing, 1 Body, 1 Killer: In US University, Bangladeshi Students' Murder
content: Zamil Limon and Nahida Bristy, both 27-year-old Bangladeshi doctoral students at the University of South Florida (USF), were reported missing on April 17.

title: Adani Foundation Sets Up Garment Centre To Empower Women In Madhya Pradesh
content: The Adani Foundation has set up a garment skills and production centre in Badarwas block of Shivpuri district in Madhya Pradesh under its CSR initiative to empower women.

title: J&K parties back Engineer Rashid over bail to visit ailing father
content: a NIA court rejected the jailed baramulla MP’s bail plea to meet his critically ill father . his son said his grandfather was on ventilator support in the ICU of Srinagar’s SMHS Hospital .

title: Ladakh lifts bar on lower-qualified govt staff from applying for graduate-level posts
content: ladakh lieutenant governor lifted regulations that barred govt employees from applying for graduate-level posts . a candidate selected for a lower-qualification job was required to submit an affidavit declaring that they were only a Class 10 or Class 12 pass . the embargo was “unjustified” and “restrictive,” but imposing limits on maximum qualifications barred candidates from future employment .

title: Relief for Farooq as Srinagar court stays proceedings against him in JKCA scam case
content: a sessions court stayed a magisterial court’s order allowing framing of charges against national conference president Dr Farooq Abdullah in the jammu and Kashmir cricket association scam case . the additional sessions judge ordered the operation of a chief judicial magistrate’s March 2 directive, and the subsequent proceedings initiated by the trial magistrate on March 30 to be kept in abeyance till the next date of hearing .

content: A writer at Times of India, I cover the stories that live in the gaps — between a screen and real life, between what we're sold and what we actually feel. I try bring curiosity and candour to everything I write, whether I'm digging into global affairs, modern relationships, or the quieter anxieties of everyday life. Read More
